query:
You are a SaaS pricing analyst. For each of the 10 companies below, extract their CURRENT pricing structure from their public pricing pages ({domain}/pricing, {domain}/plans), G2, Capterra, and reverse-engineering posts.

Companies:
  1. name="Coval", website="https://coval.ai"
  2. name="SiliconFlow", website="https://siliconflow.com"
  3. name="Together AI", website="https://together.ai"
  4. name="Workato", website="https://orkato.com"
  5. name="Northflank", website="https://northflank.com"
  6. name="Botpress", website="https://botpress.com"
  7. name="Hugging Face", website="https://huggingface.co"
  8. name="Altair AI Studio", website="https://altair.com"
  9. name="Fermyon", website="https://fermyon.com"
  10. name="AWS Bedrock", website="https://aws.amazon.com"

For EACH company extract:
- pricing_model_kind: one of Freemium, Subscription, Usage-based, Enterprise, Hybrid
- free_plan: true/false
- starts_at_usd: lowest paid tier monthly USD (0 if free plan only, null if Enterprise-only / contact sales)
- mention: short UI tagline e.g. 'Starts at $20/user/mo' or 'Contact sales' or 'Free + paid plans from $8/mo'
- tiers: array of {name, price_monthly_usd, price_annual_usd, features (3-6 strings), target}. Include ALL public tiers (e.g. Free, Pro, Business, Enterprise). For Enterprise tiers without public price, set price_monthly_usd=null and target='Enterprise'.
- recent_changes: any pricing change in last 12 months (date + source). Leave blank if none.

Omit any field you cannot verify — do NOT invent prices. Return one object per company in the SAME order as the input list. Preserve company names EXACTLY.
answer:
{"executive_summary": "- **Pricing Model Divergence**: 7 of 10 companies use Hybrid models (freemium + usage + enterprise), reflecting AI infrastructure's inability to fit pure subscription economics -> Buyers must model variable spend, not just flat fees.
- **Free Tier Erosion**: Together AI killed its free plan in July 2025, requiring a $5 minimum credit purchase, signaling that inference-hosting free tiers are economically unsustainable at scale -> Evaluate vendor free tiers for durability before building dependencies.
- **Botpress Price Doubling**: Botpress restructured from per-message + separate AI spend to per-conversation billing in May 2026, raising Plus from $89/mo to $150/mo (annual) and Team from $495/mo to $750/mo -> Lock in annual rates before further increases; the bundled-AI-spend model may still reduce total cost for high-message-volume users.
- **Enterprise Opacity Dominates**: Workato, Altair AI Studio, and Coval's Enterprise tiers lack public pricing, forcing procurement teams to rely on third-party estimates ranging from $10K to $70K/yr -> Budget 20-40% above third-party floor estimates for negotiation room.
- **Usage-Based Per-Token Dominance**: AWS Bedrock, SiliconFlow, and Together AI price per million tokens, with input costs spanning $0.003 to $1.60 per 1M tokens depending on model -> Model selection drives cost more than vendor choice; compare per-model rates, not just vendor brands.
- **Infrastructure Freemium Viability**: Northflank and Fermyon sustain genuine free tiers (2 services/1 DB and 5 active spins respectively) because their marginal compute cost approaches zero at low usage -> These free tiers are reliable for prototyping but will not scale to production workloads.
- **Acquisition-Driven Pricing Shifts**: Fermyon (acquired by Akamai, Nov 2025) and Altair AI Studio (acquired by Siemens, 2025) now reflect parent-company go-to-market motions, with potential future integration into broader platform bundles -> Monitor post-acquisition pricing pages for bundle discounts or forced migrations.
- **Hugging Face's Per-Seat Enterprise Ramp**: Hugging Face prices Enterprise at $50/user/mo, creating steep cost escalation for large ML teams compared to Pro at $9/mo -> Consolidate users into Team ($20/user/mo) where SSO and audit logs suffice before upgrading to Enterprise.

---", "1_coval_hybrid_no_free_plan_starts_at_100_mo": "Coval is an AI voice and chat agent evaluation platform. Its pricing page at coval.ai/pricing shows three subscription tiers with usage caps and a custom Enterprise option. Notably, Coval offers **no permanent free plan** - only 7-day trials on lower tiers.

| Tier | Monthly (USD) | Annual (USD/mo) | Key Features | Target |
|------|--------------|-----------------|--------------|--------|
| Starter | $100 | ~$80 (20% discount) | 100 simulation mins/mo, 1,000 monitored calls/mo, 30-day trace retention, Unlimited seats, Basic voice models, Community + email support | Teams starting agent evaluation |
| Growth | $500 | ~$400 | 1,000 simulation mins/mo, 10,000 monitored calls/mo, 90-day trace retention, Advanced voice models, Google SSO/RBAC/Audit logs, 99% uptime SLA | Growing voice/chat-agent teams in production |
| Enterprise | $4,500 (starting) | Custom | Custom simulation mins + monitored calls, Custom voice models, SAML SSO/SCIM/RBAC, VPC/Data residency/BYO storage, White-glove onboarding, Custom SLA up to 99.99% | Regulated industries, high-volume orgs |

- **pricing_model_kind**: Hybrid
- **free_plan**: false
- **starts_at_usd**: 100
- **mention**: "Starts at $100/mo; Enterprise from $4,500/mo"
- **recent_changes**: Tier restructuring between Feb-May 2026. SaaSworthy's February 2026 listing showed Core at $300/mo and Scale at $999/mo. Current site shows Starter $100/mo, Growth $500/mo, Enterprise $4,500/mo - a restructured lineup with a lower entry point and renamed tiers ([1_coval_hybrid_no_free_plan_starts_at_100_mo[0]] [15], [1_coval_hybrid_no_free_plan_starts_at_100_mo[1]] [51]).

---", "2_siliconflow_hybrid_free_credits_pay_as_you_go": "SiliconFlow is a Chinese AI inference platform offering 200+ optimized LLMs and multimodal models via API. Pricing is transparently pay-as-you-go with free signup credits, no subscription tiers, and an Enterprise volume-discount option.

| Tier | Monthly (USD) | Annual (USD) | Key Features | Target |
|------|--------------|--------------|--------------|--------|
| Free Credits | $0 | N/A | $1 free credits on signup, Full model catalog access, No minimum commitments, Configurable monthly spending limits, No payment method required | Developers evaluating the platform |
| Pay-as-you-Go (Serverless) | Variable (usage) | N/A | Per-token LLM pricing (e.g. DeepSeek-V4-Pro: $1.60/M input, $3.48/M output), Per-image (e.g. Z-Image-Turbo: $0.005/image), Per-video pricing, Cached-input auto-discounts, Postpaid billing with spending limits | Individual developers, startups, SMBs |
| Enterprise | null | Custom | Volume discounts, Custom pricing plans, Dedicated support, Fine-tuning services, Reserved GPU capacity | Enterprise |

- **pricing_model_kind**: Hybrid
- **free_plan**: true
- **starts_at_usd**: 0
- **mention**: "Free $1 credits + pay-as-you-go from $0.005/image"
- **recent_changes**: None found.

Sources: [2_siliconflow_hybrid_free_credits_pay_as_you_go[0]] [22], [2_siliconflow_hybrid_free_credits_pay_as_you_go[1]] [25], [2_siliconflow_hybrid_free_credits_pay_as_you_go[2]] [23].

---", "3_together_ai_usage_based_free_tier_discontinued_july_2025": "Together AI provides serverless inference, dedicated endpoints, fine-tuning, and GPU clusters for open-source models. A critical recent change: **the free tier was eliminated in July 2025**, replaced by a mandatory minimum $5 credit purchase. Current pricing is purely usage-based per token with no subscription plans.

| Tier | Monthly (USD) | Annual (USD) | Key Features | Target |
|------|--------------|--------------|--------------|--------|
| Pay-as-you-Go (Serverless) | Variable (per-token) | N/A | Per-token pricing across 200+ models, No minimum commitments, Serverless + dedicated endpoints, Fine-tuning support, GPU clusters available | Individual developers and teams |
| Dedicated Endpoints | Variable | N/A | Dedicated GPU infrastructure, Custom model hosting, Higher throughput guarantees | Production workloads |
| Enterprise | null | Custom | Custom pricing and volume discounts, Dedicated support, Startup accelerator (up to $50K credits), Committed capacity options | Enterprise |

- **pricing_model_kind**: Usage-based
- **free_plan**: false
- **starts_at_usd**: 5 (minimum credit purchase; no subscription tier)
- **mention**: "From $5 credits; per-token pricing; Enterprise contact sales"
- **recent_changes**: (1) Free tier discontinued July 2025 - minimum $5 credit purchase now required ([3_together_ai_usage_based_free_tier_discontinued_july_2025[0]] [75]). (2) Model pricing updates effective April 15, 2026 - e.g. google/gemma-3n-E4B-it input pricing increased from $0.02 to $0.06 per 1M tokens ([3_together_ai_usage_based_free_tier_discontinued_july_2025[1]] [57]).

Sources: [3_together_ai_usage_based_free_tier_discontinued_july_2025[2]] [2], [3_together_ai_usage_based_free_tier_discontinued_july_2025[3]] [3], [3_together_ai_usage_based_free_tier_discontinued_july_2025[4]] [74].

---", "4_workato_enterprise_no_public_pricing": "Workato is the #1 iPaaS platform for enterprise automation. Its pricing is entirely custom and quote-based, combining a platform edition fee with usage-based billing scaled by recipe execution volume. No prices are published on workato.com/pricing, which simply states "Flexible pricing built for scalability."

| Tier | Monthly (USD) | Annual (USD) | Key Features | Target |
|------|--------------|--------------|--------------|--------|
| Starter | ~$833/mo (est. ~$10K/yr) | ~$10,000 (est.) | Basic workflow automation, Limited connectors, Platform edition fee + usage, Small team deployment | Small teams automating essential workflows |
| Professional | ~$2,083-$3,750/mo (est. $25K-$45K/yr) | $25,000-$45,000 (est.) | Advanced connectors and recipes, Multi-department deployment, Higher usage limits, Enhanced support | Mid-size companies |
| Enterprise | null | Custom | Unlimited connectors/recipes, Advanced security and governance, Dedicated support, SSO/SAML, Custom integrations | Enterprise |
| UK G-Cloud | ~$3,333-$5,833/mo | GBP 40,000-70,000/yr | Regulatory compliance, Data governance, Automated controls | UK public sector |

- **pricing_model_kind**: Enterprise
- **free_plan**: false
- **starts_at_usd**: null
- **mention**: "Contact sales; estimated from ~$10K/yr"
- **recent_changes**: None found.

Note: All prices above Starter/Professional are third-party estimates, not official. Workato's docs confirm: "A platform edition fee, which determines access to platform capabilities. A usage fee, which scales based on your usage volume." ([4_workato_enterprise_no_public_pricing[0]] [61]). Estimates from [4_workato_enterprise_no_public_pricing[1]] [59], [4_workato_enterprise_no_public_pricing[2]] [60], [4_workato_enterprise_no_public_pricing[3]] [62].

---", "5_northflank_hybrid_free_sandbox_usage_based_compute": "Northflank is a unified developer platform for building, deploying, and managing applications. It offers a genuine free Sandbox tier, a consumption-based Pay-as-you-go tier with no seat fees, and a custom Enterprise option.

| Tier | Monthly (USD) | Annual (USD) | Key Features | Target |
|------|--------------|--------------|--------------|--------|
| Sandbox | $0 | $0 | 2 free services, 1 free database, 2 free cron jobs, Always-on compute, 2 projects limit | Individual developers and evaluation |
| Pay-as-you-go | Variable (usage) | N/A | Unlimited projects/services/jobs/addons, Fine-grained RBAC + autoscaling, 6+ managed regions + 600 BYOC regions, CPU $0.01667/vCPU/hr + Memory $0.00833/GB/hr, GPU from $0.80/hr (L4) to $3.00/hr (RTX PRO 6000), Pro-rated per-second billing | Solo developers and startups |
| Enterprise | null | Custom | SSO/SAML/OIDC, Comprehensive audit logging, Global HA/DR, Up to 50K developers per tenant, Volume discounts + annual commitments, White-labeling + self-deployable control plane | Large organizations |

- **pricing_model_kind**: Hybrid
- **free_plan**: true
- **starts_at_usd**: 0
- **mention**: "Free sandbox + pay-as-you-go from $0.01667/vCPU/hr"
- **recent_changes**: None found.

Sources: [5_northflank_hybrid_free_sandbox_usage_based_compute[0]] [20], [5_northflank_hybrid_free_sandbox_usage_based_compute[1]] [80], [5_northflank_hybrid_free_sandbox_usage_based_compute[2]] [19].

---", "6_botpress_hybrid_major_may_2026_pricing_overhaul": "Botpress is an AI agent platform for building and deploying conversational bots. In May 2026, it executed a significant pricing restructuring: shifting from per-message billing with separate AI spend to per-conversation billing with AI spend bundled. This raised headline prices but simplified cost predictability.

| Tier | Monthly (USD) | Annual (USD/mo) | Key Features | Target |
|------|--------------|-----------------|--------------|--------|
| Free | $0 | $0 | 100 conversations/mo, 3 seats, 3 AI agents, Botpress Desk (AI helpdesk), Community support | Individual developers, small teams |
| Plus | $189 | $150 | 250 conversations/mo, Unlimited AI agents, WhatsApp + whitelabel webchat, Custom domain Help Center, 100K table rows + 1GB vector + 10GB file storage, Technical support via live chat | Solo operators and small groups |
| Team | $939 | $750 | 1,500 conversations/mo, Unlimited seats, Teams + routing rules + RBAC, Team analytics + real-time collaboration, 500K table rows + 5GB vector + 50GB file storage, Overage packs at $0.50/convo | Support operations scaling |
| Enterprise | null | Custom | Custom conversation volume + pricing, Dedicated support engineer + formal SLA, Custom storage + dedicated voice channel, Audit logs + DPA + BAA (HIPAA), Custom data retention/residency | Enterprise |

- **pricing_model_kind**: Hybrid
- **free_plan**: true
- **starts_at_usd**: 150 (annual billing) or 189 (monthly)
- **mention**: "Free + paid plans from $150/mo (annual)"
- **recent_changes**: Major restructuring on **May 14, 2026**: (1) Shifted from per-message + separate AI spend to per-conversation billing with AI spend bundled; (2) Eliminated $10/mo per-bot add-on - unlimited bots now included on all paid tiers; (3) Plus rose from $89/mo to $150/mo (annual) and Team from $495/mo to $750/mo (annual); (4) Added "Always Alive" (no cold starts) as standard on paid plans; (5) Introduced Desk (AI helpdesk) and Built For You (managed service from $1,650/mo) products. Old workspaces grandfathered into previous pricing. Source: [6_botpress_hybrid_major_may_2026_pricing_overhaul[0]] [66].

Sources: [6_botpress_hybrid_major_may_2026_pricing_overhaul[1]] [52], [6_botpress_hybrid_major_may_2026_pricing_overhaul[2]] [67], [6_botpress_hybrid_major_may_2026_pricing_overhaul[3]] [64].

---", "7_hugging_face_hybrid_free_pro_from_9_mo": "Hugging Face operates the central open-source ML Hub (models, datasets, Spaces) with a layered pricing structure spanning free community access, individual Pro, collaborative Team, and Enterprise Hub with compliance features. Usage-based add-ons for storage and compute sit alongside subscription tiers.

| Tier | Monthly (USD) | Annual (USD) | Key Features | Target |
|------|--------------|--------------|--------------|--------|
| Free | $0 | $0 | Open-source Hub access, Free CPU Basic + ZeroGPU Spaces, Base storage allocation, Standard inference credits, Git-based collaboration + community support | Hobbyists, students, open-source contributors |
| Pro | $9/user | N/A (monthly) | 10x private storage + 2x public storage, 20x inference credits, 8x ZeroGPU quota with highest priority, Spaces Dev Mode (SSH/VS Code), Personal blog + PRO badge | Individual practitioners and researchers |
| Team | $20/user | N/A (monthly) | SSO (SAML + OIDC), Storage Regions (data location), Audit Logs, Resource Groups, Repository usage Analytics, Advanced compute for Spaces | Growing teams, startups, university labs |
| Enterprise | $50/user | Custom (annual commitments) | Highest storage/bandwidth/API rate limits, SCIM provisioning, Advanced security + access controls, Managed billing + annual commitments, Legal/Compliance processes, Dedicated support + custom onboarding | Large enterprises |

- **pricing_model_kind**: Hybrid
- **free_plan**: true
- **starts_at_usd**: 9
- **mention**: "Free + Pro from $9/mo, Team $20/user/mo, Enterprise $50/user/mo"
- **recent_changes**: None found in the last 12 months.

Usage-based add-ons: Storage from $12/TB/mo (public) and $18/TB/mo (private), with volume discounts down to $8/TB/mo at 500TB+. Spaces hardware from $0.40/hr (T4 small) to $23.50/hr (8x L40S). Inference Endpoints from $0.033/hr (CPU) to $74.00/hr (8x B200).

Sources: [7_hugging_face_hybrid_free_pro_from_9_mo[0]] [33], [7_hugging_face_hybrid_free_pro_from_9_mo[1]] [34], [7_hugging_face_hybrid_free_pro_from_9_mo[2]] [69], [7_hugging_face_hybrid_free_pro_from_9_mo[3]] [72].

---", "8_altair_ai_studio_hybrid_free_personal_quote_based_standard_academic": "Altair AI Studio (formerly RapidMiner) is a data science and ML development platform, now part of Siemens Xcelerator following Siemens' acquisition of Altair. It offers a free Personal Edition limited to 10,000 rows and two paid editions requiring annual subscription quotes.

| Tier | Monthly (USD) | Annual (USD) | Key Features | Target |
|------|--------------|--------------|--------------|--------|
| Personal Edition | $0 | $0 | Data Connectivity, Machine Learning Algorithms, Visualizations, Scripting, Extensibility (limited to 10,000 rows), No Auto Model/Turbo Prep/Interactive Analysis/Radoop | Consumers and businesses for individual exploration |
| Standard Edition | null | null (annual subscription, request quote) | Data Connectivity, Machine Learning Algorithms, Auto Model, Turbo Prep, Interactive Analysis, Radoop Extension, Full dataset size | Consumers and businesses (full commercial use) |
| Academic Edition | null | null (annual subscription for university computers) | Same as Standard Edition, Licensed for university-owned computers, Student Edition available free via Altair Licensing | Universities and academic institutions |

- **pricing_model_kind**: Hybrid
- **free_plan**: true
- **starts_at_usd**: null (Standard Edition requires quote; no public price)
- **mention**: "Free Personal Edition; Standard/Academic contact sales"
- **recent_changes**: Altair acquired by Siemens (completed 2025); product now offered through Siemens Xcelerator platform. No specific pricing change documented ([8_altair_ai_studio_hybrid_free_personal_quote_based_standard_academic[0]] [46], Siemens RapidMiner).

---", "9_fermyon_hybrid_free_starter_growth_at_19_38_mo": "Fermyon provides serverless WebAssembly (Spin) application hosting on its cloud platform, acquired by Akamai in November 2025. It offers a free Starter tier for development, a low-cost Growth tier, and a custom Enterprise option.

| Tier | Monthly (USD) | Annual (USD) | Key Features | Target |
|------|--------------|--------------|--------------|--------|
| Starter | $0 | $0 | 5 active spins (applications), 500 requests/min per spin, 1 GB key-value store, Custom domains, Community support | Individual developers getting started |
| Growth | $19.38 | N/A | 25 active spins, 5,000 requests/min per spin, 5 GB key-value store, Priority support, Higher usage limits across all resources | Growing teams and projects |
| Enterprise | null | Custom | Custom usage limits, Dedicated support + SLA, Advanced security and compliance, VPC/private deployment, Custom integrations | Enterprise organizations |

- **pricing_model_kind**: Hybrid
- **free_plan**: true
- **starts_at_usd**: 0
- **mention**: "Free starter + Growth from $19.38/mo"
- **recent_changes**: Fermyon acquired by Akamai in November 2025; pricing page now references Akamai Functions. No pricing rate changes documented post-acquisition (Akamai Press Release, [9_fermyon_hybrid_free_starter_growth_at_19_38_mo[0]] [35]).

Sources: [9_fermyon_hybrid_free_starter_growth_at_19_38_mo[0]] [35], [9_fermyon_hybrid_free_starter_growth_at_19_38_mo[1]] [39], [9_fermyon_hybrid_free_starter_growth_at_19_38_mo[2]] [36].

---", "10_aws_bedrock_usage_based_per_token_from_0_003_1k_input_tokens": "Amazon Bedrock is AWS's managed foundation model service providing access to models from Anthropic (Claude), Meta (Llama), Amazon (Nova/Titan), Mistral, Cohere, and others. Pricing is purely consumption-based with no subscription tiers. Three pricing models serve different workload patterns.

| Pricing Model | Monthly (USD) | Key Features | Target |
|---------------|--------------|--------------|--------|
| On-Demand (Pay-as-you-go) | Variable per-token | Input tokens $0.003-$0.075 per 1K (model-dependent), Output tokens $0.015-$0.30 per 1K, No minimum commitments, All foundation models available, No upfront cost | All customers; experimentation and variable workloads |
| Provisioned Throughput | From ~$21-$63/hr | Committed capacity (Dedicated Units), Guaranteed throughput for production, Hourly billing, Customized DUs by model, Suitable for latency-sensitive apps | Production deployments requiring guaranteed throughput |
| Batch Mode | Discounted on-demand rates | Cost-efficient bulk processing, Discounted rates vs on-demand, Asynchronous processing, Suitable for non-interactive workloads | Bulk processing and batch inference |
| Flex Tier | Discounted on-demand rates | Discounted standard pricing, For workloads tolerating delayed processing, Lower priority queue | Cost-optimized flexible workloads |

- **pricing_model_kind**: Usage-based
- **free_plan**: false (no dedicated Bedrock free tier; AWS Free Tier may provide limited trial access)
- **starts_at_usd**: null
- **mention**: "Pay-as-you-go per token from $0.003/1K input tokens"
- **recent_changes**: Flex tier introduced offering discounted standard pricing for flexible workloads. Model pricing updates ongoing (source: [10_aws_bedrock_usage_based_per_token_from_0_003_1k_input_tokens[0]] [54], [10_aws_bedrock_usage_based_per_token_from_0_003_1k_input_tokens[1]] [40], [10_aws_bedrock_usage_based_per_token_from_0_003_1k_input_tokens[2]] [41]).

---", "synthesis_cross_cutting_pricing_patterns_across_ai_dev_platforms": "### Dimension 1: Pricing Model Distribution

The 10 companies cluster into three pricing model families: **Hybrid** (7 of 10), **Usage-based** (2: Together AI, AWS Bedrock), and **Enterprise** (1: Workato). The dominance of Hybrid models reflects a fundamental tension in AI infrastructure - inference compute costs are variable and hard to predict, so vendors layer usage-based billing on top of subscription commitments to protect margins while offering predictable entry points.

### Dimension 2: Free Tier Viability Spectrum

| Free Tier Durability | Companies | Risk Level |
|---------------------|-----------|------------|
| Durable (infrastructure-free) | Hugging Face, Altair AI Studio, Northflank, Fermyon | Low - marginal cost near zero |
| Fragile (inference-subsidized) | SiliconFlow ($1 credits), Botpress (100 convos) | Medium - can be tightened |
| Eliminated | Together AI (killed July 2025) | High - precedent for removal |
| Never existed | Coval, Workato, AWS Bedrock | N/A |

The key insight: free tiers that subsidize GPU inference (Together AI, SiliconFlow, Botpress) are economically fragile. Together AI's July 2025 removal of its free tier proves that inference-subsidized free plans are a customer acquisition cost, not a permanent offering. Infrastructure-free free tiers (Northflank's 2-service sandbox, Fermyon's 5-spin starter) are structurally sustainable because marginal compute cost at minimal usage approaches zero.

### Dimension 3: Price Transparency vs. Scale

A clear inverse relationship exists between price transparency and target market scale. Hugging Face and Botpress publish exact per-seat/per-conversation numbers. Workato and Altair AI Studio reveal nothing publicly, forcing reliance on third-party estimates. AWS Bedrock and SiliconFlow publish per-token rates but make total monthly cost prediction dependent on model selection and usage volume - a different form of opacity.

### Dimension 4: Recent Change Velocity

Three companies underwent significant pricing changes in the last 12 months: Botpress (May 2026 overhaul), Together AI (July 2025 free tier elimination), and Coval (tier restructuring early 2026). All three changes moved pricing toward higher predictability for the vendor - bundled billing, minimum commitments, or simplified tier structures. This suggests the AI infrastructure market is maturing from "land at any cost" toward sustainable unit economics, a trend buyers should expect to continue.

---"}
